detect-hang.prompt: Run hang detection scan now. Read the 'hangDetection' config from this job definition for per-agent thresholds. Scan all VO agent lock files under Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/state/agents/*/lock. For each lock, check if the age exceeds that agent's hangThresholdMinutes (default 60). For hung jobs: walk the process tree, capture py-spy stack traces for Python processes, classify the hang, decide whether to kill (only if age > killThresholdMinutes), log the incident, and file GitHub issues for new patterns. Skip any agents listed in 'exclude'. Output a scan summary at the end. IMPORTANT: Your output MUST use ASCII-only characters. Use -- instead of em dashes, -> instead of arrows (right arrow), <- instead of left arrows, ... instead of ellipsis, and straight quotes instead of curly quotes.
detect-hang.description: Scans for hung VO agent jobs, diagnoses via process tree and py-spy, kills if safe, logs incidents

detect-scrum-master.prompt: Run hang detection scan targeting ONLY the scrum-master agent. Use hangThresholdMinutes=30 and killThresholdMinutes=60 for this scan (override the config defaults). Scan ONLY the lock file at Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/state/agents/scrum-master/lock. If age >= 30 minutes: walk the process tree, capture py-spy stack traces for Python processes, classify the hang, decide whether to kill (only if age > 60 min), log the incident, and file GitHub issues for new patterns. Output scan summary. IMPORTANT: Your output MUST use ASCII-only characters. Use -- instead of em dashes, -> instead of arrows (right arrow), <- instead of left arrows, ... instead of ellipsis, and straight quotes instead of curly quotes.
detect-scrum-master.description: One-off hang detection for scrum-master only with 20min hang / 60min kill thresholds

daily-report.prompt: Generate the daily 5W incident report. (1) Parse audit logs from Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/output/audit/*.jsonl for the past 7 days to extract run durations per agent/job. (2) Collect all hang incidents from Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/output/hang-scout/incidents/ in the last 24 hours. (3) Collect bug-autopilot performance data: read all run-*-summary.json files from the bugAutopilotPerf.logsPath for the last 24 hours (today) and last 7 days (baseline). Merge per_step_aggregate data across runs. Flag outliers (max > outlierMultiplier * avg), threshold breaches (p95 > stepThresholds), trend increases (> trendAlertPercent vs 7-day avg), and slow bugs (> 2x run average). (4) If no meaningful work was done (no incidents in the last 24 hours AND no interesting runtime trends -- no alerts triggered), do NOT generate an HTML report -- just output 'Hang Scout: no incidents or alerts in the last 24 hours.' and exit. Only generate a full report when there are incidents to report or runtime trend alerts were triggered. You MUST use the fixed template at Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/templates/hang-scout-daily-report.html. Read the template file, then replace all {{PLACEHOLDER}} values with actual data. Do NOT redesign the layout, change colors, rearrange sections, or generate HTML from scratch. Replace {{VO_SUBTITLE}} with: Agent: Hang Scout | Job: daily-report | Start: <PST timestamp when job started> | Complete: <PST timestamp when report is generated>. For Chart.js data, replace {{TREND_LABELS}}, {{TREND_DATASETS}}, {{BAR_LABELS}}, {{BAR_AVG_7D}}, {{BAR_AVG_24H}} with JSON arrays of the computed data. Save to output/hang-scout/daily-report-YYYYMMDD.html and daily-report-latest.html. Open in Edge.
daily-report.description: Generates daily 5W incident report with bug-autopilot performance analysis
